steps: Rinse lentils and cook in 2.5 cups water with bay leaf until tender (20-25 min). Drain.
Season chicken with cumin, salt, and pepper. Grill or pan-sear until cooked through (6-7 min per side).
Sauté onion and bell pepper in olive oil until softened (5 min). Add garlic, cook 1 min.
Slice chicken. Combine lentils, vegetables, and chicken in bowl.
Drizzle with lime juice, top with cilantro. Serve warm.
Cook lentils in 2 cups water until tender (20-25 min). Drain and cool slightly.
Brown ground beef in skillet, breaking into crumbles (6-8 min). Season with oregano, garlic, salt, pepper.
Combine lentils, beef, cucumber, tomatoes, and red onion in bowl.
Drizzle with lemon juice and olive oil. Top with feta.
Toss gently and serve at room temperature or chilled.
Cook lentils in 2 cups water until tender (20-25 min). Drain.
Marinate pork slices in 1 tbsp soy sauce, ginger, and garlic for 10 min.
Stir-fry pork in sesame oil over high heat until cooked through (4-5 min).
Add bok choy, cook 2 min until wilted.
Combine lentils and pork mixture. Drizzle with remaining soy sauce and rice vinegar.
Top with green onions and sesame seeds.
Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger in 1 tsp oil until softened (5 min).
Add turmeric, curry powder, and garam masala. Cook 1 min until fragrant.
Add both lentils and 2.5 cups water. Bring to boil, reduce heat, simmer 20-25 min.
Stir in tomato and coconut milk. Cook 5 min more.
Add spinach, stir until wilted (2 min).
Season with salt. Serve hot.
Cook brown rice according to package directions (about 45 min).
Season chicken with ginger, garlic, and 1 tbsp soy sauce. Pan-sear until cooked through (6-7 min per side). Slice.
Steam broccoli and carrots until tender-crisp (5-6 min).
Combine rice, chicken, and vegetables in bowl.
Mix remaining soy sauce, sesame oil, and rice vinegar. Drizzle over bowl.
Top with green onions.
Cook brown rice according to package directions.
Brown ground beef with cumin and chili powder (6-8 min). Drain excess fat.
Stir in salsa and corn. Heat through (2 min).
Combine rice, beef mixture, and refried beans in bowl.
Top with avocado slices, cilantro, and lime juice.
Serve warm.
Cook brown rice according to package directions.
Season pork with oregano, garlic, lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Grill or pan-sear (5-6 min per side).
Slice pork into strips.
Combine rice, pork, tomatoes, cucumber, and olives in bowl.
Drizzle with olive oil and remaining lemon juice. Top with feta.
Serve at room temperature.
Cook brown rice and lentils separately according to package directions.
Heat 1 tsp oil, add cumin seeds until they sizzle (30 sec).
Add onion, garlic, ginger. Sauté until softened (4 min).
Add turmeric and curry powder. Cook 1 min.
Stir in tomato, cook 3 min. Add cooked lentils, mix well.
Serve lentil mixture over rice, top with yogurt.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss sweet potato, carrots, and parsnips with half the olive oil, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Roast vegetables 25-30 min, turning halfway, until tender and caramelized.
Season chicken with garlic, lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Pan-sear or grill until cooked through (6-7 min per side).
Slice chicken. Combine with roasted vegetables.
Drizzle with remaining olive oil and lemon juice. Top with olives and parsley.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss vegetables with 1 tsp oil, salt, and pepper. Roast 25-30 min.
Brown ground beef with onion (5-6 min). Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 min.
Add turmeric, curry powder, and garam masala. Cook 2 min more.
Combine roasted vegetables with spiced beef.
Garnish with fresh cilantro. Serve warm.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Roast vegetables with 1 tsp oil at 425°F for 25-30 min.
Marinate pork in soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and rice vinegar for 10 min.
Stir-fry pork in sesame oil over high heat until cooked through (4-5 min).
Combine roasted vegetables and pork.
Drizzle with any remaining marinade (heated). Top with green onions and sesame seeds.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss vegetables with cumin, chili powder, garlic, salt, and 1 tsp oil.
Roast 25-30 min until tender and caramelized.
Cook lentils in 2 cups water until tender (20-25 min). Drain.
Combine roasted vegetables and lentils in bowl.
Drizzle with lime juice. Top with avocado, cilantro, and pumpkin seeds.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
Cook lentils in 2 cups water until tender (20-25 min). Drain.
Season chicken with turmeric, curry powder, and garam masala. Pan-sear until cooked through (6-7 min per side). Slice.
Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger until softened (5 min). Add tomato, cook 3 min.
Stir in cooked lentils and coconut milk. Heat through.
Top with sliced chicken and cilantro. Serve hot.
Cook brown rice according to package directions.
Marinate pork in soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and rice vinegar for 10 min.
Stir-fry pork in sesame oil over high heat (4-5 min). Remove.
Stir-fry snap peas and bell pepper 3 min until crisp-tender.
Combine rice, pork, and vegetables. Drizzle with any remaining marinade.
Top with green onions and sesame seeds.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss vegetables with oregano, garlic, salt, pepper, and 1 tsp oil. Roast 25-30 min.
Brown ground beef in skillet (6-8 min). Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
Combine roasted vegetables and beef in bowl.
Top with olives, feta, and parsley.
Drizzle with remaining lemon juice. Serve warm.
Prepare steak marinade: combine olive oil, minced garlic, cumin, 1/4 tsp salt, and juice from half the lime. Add to zip-top bag with steak, massage to coat. Refrigerate 30 minutes.
Cook rice according to package directions using chicken broth instead of water for added flavor.
While rice cooks, make pico de gallo: dice tomato and half the onion, combine with remaining lime juice, 1/4 tsp salt, and chopped cilantro.
Rinse and drain black beans. Thaw corn kernels.
Heat large skillet over medium-high until very hot. Cook steak 3-5 minutes per side until deeply browned and caramelized. Rest 5 minutes.
Slice steak thinly against the grain. Optional: sauté remaining onion slices in skillet with meat drippings.
Build bowls: 1 cup rice base, top with 1/4 cup each beans, corn, pico de gallo, sliced steak, cilantro, and a dollop of sour cream.
recipes.chef_notes: Use chicken broth instead of water for rice - makes a huge difference. Slice steak against the grain for tenderness. Add avocado, sautéed peppers, or cheese as desired. Freezes well.

steps: Heat 1 Tbsp vegetable oil in large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Scramble eggs with a pinch of salt, then pour into hot skillet. Cook until just set, stirring occasionally. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil to skillet. Sauté carrots 2-3 min until slightly softened.
Add garlic, ginger, and white parts of green onions. Cook 1 min until fragrant.
Add cooked rice, breaking up clumps. Stir-fry 3-4 min until heated through.
Add peas and soy sauce, toss to combine. Cook 2 min.
Return eggs to skillet, break into pieces. Drizzle with sesame oil and rice vinegar.
Toss everything together. Top with green onion greens and sesame seeds.
recipes.chef_notes: Day-old rice works best - less sticky. Add sriracha or chili crisp for heat. Can add leftover cooked protein if desired. Perfect for using up leftover rice.

steps: Rinse lentils and cook in 2.5 cups water with a bay leaf until tender (20-25 min). Drain and set aside.
While lentils cook, prepare soft-boiled eggs: bring pot of water to boil, gently lower eggs, boil 6-7 min for jammy yolks. Ice bath to stop cooking.
Make dressing: whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Combine warm lentils with half the dressing. Toss to coat.
Arrange bowls: lentil base, topped with tomatoes, cucumber, red onion.
Peel and halve eggs, nestle into bowls. Sprinkle with paprika.
Top with feta, parsley, and drizzle remaining dressing. Serve warm or room temp.
recipes.chef_notes: Jammy eggs are key - 6-7 min boil then ice bath. Can substitute hard-boiled if preferred. Add kalamata olives for extra Mediterranean flair. Great served warm or cold for meal prep.

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Toss sweet potato, red potatoes, carrots, and red onion with olive oil, cumin, chili powder, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Spread vegetables on baking sheet in single layer. Roast 25-30 min, flipping halfway, until caramelized and tender.
While vegetables roast, prepare eggs sunny-side up or over-easy in a non-stick skillet.
Divide roasted vegetables among bowls.
Top each bowl with one egg.
Squeeze lime juice over bowls, sprinkle with cilantro and queso fresco.
Serve with avocado slices on the side.
recipes.chef_notes: Eggs can be prepared any style - poached or soft-boiled also work. Add salsa or hot sauce for extra kick. Sweet and red potato combo gives nice texture variety.

steps: Hard-boil eggs: place in pot of cold water, bring to boil, reduce heat, simmer 10 min. Ice bath, peel.
Make shallow slits in eggs and coat lightly with turmeric and salt.
Heat 1 Tbsp oil in skillet. Lightly fry eggs until golden, 2 min. Remove and set aside.
In same skillet, add remaining oil. Add cumin seeds, let sizzle 30 sec.
Add sliced onion, sauté until golden brown (8-10 min). Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 min.
Add tomato, garam masala, coriander, remaining turmeric. Cook 3-4 min until tomatoes soften.
Add cooked rice, toss gently to coat with spices. Season with salt.
Transfer rice to bowls, nestle fried eggs on top.
Garnish with yogurt drizzle, cilantro, and crispy fried onions.
recipes.chef_notes: Egg masala style - frying eggs briefly gives them texture. Fried onions add crunch and sweetness. Serve with cucumber raita on side for cooling effect.

steps: Heat 1 Tbsp olive oil in large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add ground lamb, break up with spoon. Cook until browned (6-8 min), drain excess fat.
Add onion and garlic to lamb, cook 3 min until softened.
Add cumin, oregano, cinnamon, salt, and pepper. Cook 1 min until fragrant.
Remove from heat. Stir in half the mint and parsley.
Make salad: combine cucumber, tomatoes, remaining herbs, lemon juice, and 1 Tbsp olive oil.
Build bowls: rice base, topped with spiced lamb.
Add cucumber-tomato salad alongside.
Top with feta and drizzle with tzatziki. Serve warm.
recipes.chef_notes: Cinnamon adds warmth typical of Mediterranean lamb dishes. Ground lamb browns beautifully. Mint is essential - don't skip. Make your own tzatziki with yogurt, cucumber, and garlic.

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Toss sweet potato, carrots, and parsnips with 1 Tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast 25-30 min until tender and caramelized.
While vegetables roast, heat remaining oil in skillet over medium-high heat.
Add ground lamb, breaking up with spoon. Cook until browned (6-8 min). Drain excess fat.
Add onion, garlic, and ginger. Cook 3 min until softened.
Add garam masala, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and cayenne. Cook 2 min until fragrant.
Add 1/4 cup water to deglaze pan, scraping up browned bits. Simmer 3 min.
Combine roasted vegetables with spiced lamb in a large bowl.
Top with cilantro and drizzle with yogurt. Serve warm.
recipes.chef_notes: Sweet potatoes pair beautifully with warm Indian spices. Lamb adds richness. Yogurt cools the spices. Add fresh mint along with cilantro for extra brightness.

steps: Rinse lentils and cook in 2.5 cups water until tender (20-25 min). Drain and set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add ground lamb, cook until browned (6-7 min), breaking up with spoon. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 min until fragrant.
Add mushrooms, cook 3-4 min until softened.
Add bok choy, cook 2 min until wilted.
Stir in cooked lentils.
In small bowl, whisk soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and sriracha.
Pour sauce over lamb and lentils, toss to coat. Cook 2 min.
Top with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve hot.
recipes.chef_notes: Lamb and lentils make an unexpected but delicious pairing. Shiitakes add umami depth. For extra richness, add a drizzle of chili oil at the end.

recipes.recipe_name: Mexican Lamb and Root Vegetable Bowl
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Toss sweet potato, squash, and red onion with 1 Tbsp olive oil, cumin, chili powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Spread on baking sheet, roast 25-30 min until caramelized and tender.
While vegetables roast, heat remaining oil in skillet over medium-high heat.
Add ground lamb, cook 6-7 min until browned, breaking up with spoon. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic and oregano, cook 1 min.
Add chipotle powder if using, plus 2 Tbsp water. Simmer 2 min.
Combine roasted vegetables with spiced lamb.
Drizzle with lime juice, top with cilantro and queso fresco.
recipes.chef_notes: Ground lamb brings richness that pairs beautifully with sweet roasted vegetables. Chipotle adds smoky heat. Can substitute cotija for queso fresco.

metadata.restrictions: No tofu, no seafood. Weight-loss friendly (350-500 cal range).
metadata.notes: Gold Standard library complete with verified ratings, chef's notes, and cost-per-serving from Budget Bytes and other high-trust sources. All recipes tested and budget-friendly.

steps: Combine hoisin, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic in a small bowl. Set aside.
Heat cooking oil in large skillet over medium heat. Add ground chicken, crumble and cook until browned.
Add bell pepper and water chestnuts. Sauté 2 minutes.
Pour sauce mixture into skillet, mix well. Cook 2 more minutes.
Remove from heat, top with green onions and peanuts.
Serve 1/3 cup filling in each lettuce leaf. Optional: drizzle with peanut lime dressing.
external_recipes.chef_notes: Meal prep friendly - store filling and lettuce separately. Ground turkey or pork also works. Add cilantro and red pepper flakes for extra flavor.

steps: Heat oil in skillet, sauté onion 2-3 min until soft. Remove from heat.
Combine ground chicken, sautéed onion, panko, mayo, parsley, Worcestershire, salt, garlic powder, and pepper. Mix just until combined.
Divide into 4 portions (4 oz each). Shape into patties, press thumb indent in center of each.
Grill or pan-sear 4-5 min per side until internal temp reaches 165°F.
Toast buns on grill 1-2 min. Let burgers rest 3-4 min before serving.
Top with lettuce, tomato, pickles, red onion as desired.
external_recipes.chef_notes: Mayo keeps lean chicken juicy - don't skip it! Don't overmix or patties get tough. Freeze raw patties up to 3 months.

steps: Heat oil in large pot over medium. Sauté onion, celery, carrots, garlic 5 min until softened.
Add lentils, broth, water, salt, pepper, paprika, thyme. Stir to combine.
Bring to medium simmer. Add diced ham.
Simmer 30 min, stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender with slight bite.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Ladle into bowls and serve.
external_recipes.chef_notes: Use leftover ham or ham hock for deeper flavor. Lentils hold shape well. Freezes 3 months. Add spinach or kale for extra veggies.

steps: Dice onion, carrot, celery. Mince garlic.
Heat oil in large pot. Sauté veggies 5 min until softened.
Add garlic, red pepper, walnuts. Sauté 2 min over medium-low.
Add lentils, crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, oregano, basil, garlic powder, salt, sugar, pepper, broth.
Bring to boil, reduce to low, simmer semi-covered 20-25 min until lentils soft and sauce thickened.
Stir in coconut milk, simmer 5 more min. Taste, adjust salt.
external_recipes.chef_notes: Red lentils break down for thick, meaty texture. Walnuts add richness. Freezes 3 months. Serve over pasta, rice, or baked potatoes.

steps: Heat oil in large deep skillet. Sauté onion 2-3 min until translucent. Add garlic 30 sec.
Add cumin, chili powder, adobo, oregano, salt, pepper. Toast spices 30 sec.
Add lentils, black beans, tomatoes with juices, corn, broth. Stir. Cover, bring to boil, reduce to simmer 5 min.
Add rice, stir briefly. Cover, return to boil, reduce heat, simmer 20 min.
Remove from heat, rest covered 5 min. Fluff rice, stir gently.
Top with cheese and green onions. Serve with salsa, avocado, or tortilla chips.
external_recipes.chef_notes: Perfect meal prep - holds 4 days refrigerated. Use brown lentils (not red). Don't skip the rest time - rice finishes cooking off heat.

steps: Combine chili powder, paprika, cumin, cayenne, oregano, salt, pepper for taco seasoning.
Brown ground beef in skillet over medium. Drain excess fat if needed.
Add black beans and taco seasoning. Cook 2 min, stirring.
Add tomatoes, cook 3 min until they begin to break down.
Add tortilla chips, stir into mixture.
Top with cheese, cover 2 min until melted. Garnish with green onions.
external_recipes.chef_notes: Use leftover ingredients - great pantry cleaner. Chips soften like chilaquiles. Add avocado, sour cream, jalapeños, or lettuce as desired.
